description: Credentials are injected as plaintext environment variables, leaking into logs, crash dumps, and kubectl describe output.
body: Failure mode: a Secret is consumed via env valueFrom or hardcoded env, so the value appears in kubectl describe pod, process env dumps, crash reports, and child-process inheritance, and may be committed in the manifest. Detection signals: secret values visible in pod env or describe output; Secret manifest with real data in version control; logs printing config including credentials. Do not inject secrets as env on sensitive workloads. Safer fallback: mount the Secret as a file on tmpfs or use an external secrets manager via CSI, scope RBAC to the named Secret, enable etcd encryption-at-rest, and verify the value is absent from env and VCS.
